    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec", "path": "pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go", "symbol": "Middleware"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec", "path": "pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/service.go", "symbol": "SetUserPermission"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec", "path": "pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/service.go", "symbol": "SetTeamPermission"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec", "path": "pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/service.go", "symbol": "SetBuiltInRolePermission"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec", "path": "pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go", "symbol": "GetInheritedScopes"}
  ],
  "text": "The Grafana user permission model implements a three-tier role-based access control (RBAC) system with Viewer, Editor, and Admin roles. Here is the comprehensive architecture:\n\n## Role Definition Hierarchy\nThe base role definitions are in `pkg/apimachinery/identity/role_type.go` which defines the `RoleType` enum with four values: RoleNone, RoleViewer, RoleEditor, and RoleAdmin. These are re-exported in `pkg/services/org/model.go` for organizational context.\n\n## Permission Types\n`pkg/services/dashboards/dashboardaccess/dashboard_access.go` defines three permission levels as bit flags:\n- PERMISSION_VIEW (1 << 0) - Read-only access\n- PERMISSION_EDIT (1 << 1) - Write/modify access\n- PERMISSION_ADMIN (1 << 2) - Admin access including permission management\n\n## ACL Models\n`pkg/services/dashboards/models.go` contains the `DashboardACL` struct which maps these permission types to users, teams, and roles. Permission data flows through `pkg/api/dtos/acl.go` which defines `UpdateDashboardACLCommand` and related DTOs.\n\n## Action-to-Permission Mapping\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/models.go` defines action constants (ActionFoldersRead, ActionFoldersWrite, ActionDashboardsRead, ActionDashboardsWrite, etc.) that represent granular permission operations.\n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go` maps the three role tiers to sets of actions:\n- DashboardViewActions: includes ActionDashboardsRead\n- DashboardEditActions: extends ViewActions with ActionDashboardsWrite, ActionDashboardsDelete\n- DashboardAdminActions: extends EditActions with ActionDashboardsPermissionsRead, ActionDashboardsPermissionsWrite\n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go` follows the same pattern for folder permissions:\n- FolderViewActions: ActionFoldersRead\n- FolderEditActions: extends ViewActions with ActionFoldersWrite, ActionFoldersDelete\n- FolderAdminActions: extends EditActions with ActionFoldersPermissionsRead, ActionFoldersPermissionsWrite\n\n## Role Registration and Built-in Roles\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go` contains `GetBuiltinRoles()` which registers fixed roles for Admin, Editor, and Viewer with their associated permission sets. These are referenced throughout the codebase.\n\n## Permission Enforcement Middleware\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go` provides the `Middleware()` function that acts as a permission guard for HTTP endpoints. It evaluates whether a user has required permissions before allowing access.\n\n`pkg/middleware/auth.go` provides additional auth middleware functions like `SnapshotPublicModeOrCreate` and `SnapshotPublicModeOrDelete` that enforce specific permission checks.\n\n## Permissions Service Interface\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/accesscontrol.go` defines the `PermissionsService`, `FolderPermissionsService`, and `DashboardPermissionsService` interfaces. These services are implemented in `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/service.go` with methods:\n- SetUserPermission: assigns a permission to a specific user\n- SetTeamPermission: assigns a permission to a team\n- SetBuiltInRolePermission: assigns a permission to a built-in role (Viewer/Editor/Admin)\n\nThe API layer in `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/api.go` exposes HTTP endpoints for managing these permissions.\n\n## Folder and Dashboard Permissions APIs\n`pkg/api/dashboard_permission.go` and `pkg/api/folder_permission.go` provide HTTP endpoints to:\n- Get permissions (using folder/dashboard-specific permission service)\n- Update permissions with validation\n- Convert permission objects for API responses\n\n## Permission Inheritance\n`pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go` contains the critical `GetInheritedScopes()` function which implements permission inheritance from parent folders to child dashboards and subfolders. This function queries the folder service to get ancestors and builds a chain of inherited permission scopes.\n\n`pkg/services/searchV2/auth.go` uses inherited scopes for search result filtering, checking permissions across the folder hierarchy.\n\n`pkg/api/annotations.go` applies inherited scopes when resolving permissions for annotations linked to dashboards.\n\n## Folder Hierarchy Support\n`pkg/services/folder/service.go` and `pkg/services/folder/model.go` provide folder hierarchy operations. The folder service's `GetParents()` method is used by `GetInheritedScopes()` to walk up the folder tree and collect inherited permission scopes.\n\nThis architecture enables Grafana to:\n1. Define granular permission actions mapped to three role tiers\n2. Enforce permissions via middleware on all endpoints\n3. Allow per-resource permission overrides for users, teams, and basic roles\n4. Inherit permissions from parent folders to contained resources\n5. Validate and update permissions through a centralized permissions service"
}

conversation_events.text:
# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

Use `sg_list_repos` to discover available repositories before searching.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

# Grafana User Permission Model Role Coverage Audit

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in grafana/grafana that define and enforce the three-tier permission model (Viewer/Editor/Admin): the role-to-permission mapping, the permission guard middleware, and the folder/dashboard permission inheritance.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the compliance domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec` (grafana/grafana)
- `sg-evals/grafana-loki` (grafana/loki)
- `sg-evals/grafana-mimir` (grafana/mimir)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you audit the Grafana user permission model. Let me start by exploring the available repositories and finding the relevant Go source files.
Now let me search for additional related files on permission enforcement and basic role definitions:

Based on my comprehensive search through the Grafana codebase, I've identified all the key files that define and enforce the three-tier permission model (Viewer/Editor/Admin). Let me create the answer file:

I've successfully completed the audit of the Grafana user permission model. The analysis has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json`.

## Summary

I've identified **25 key Go source files** that define and enforce Grafana's three-tier permission model (Viewer/Editor/Admin):

### Core Components Found:

1. **Role Definitions**: `pkg/apimachinery/identity/role_type.go` - Defines the RoleType enum (RoleNone, RoleViewer, RoleEditor, RoleAdmin)

2. **Permission Types**: `pkg/services/dashboards/dashboardaccess/dashboard_access.go` - Defines three permission levels (PERMISSION_VIEW, PERMISSION_EDIT, PERMISSION_ADMIN)

3. **Action-to-Permission Mapping**: 
   - `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go` - Maps role tiers to dashboard actions
   - `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go` - Maps role tiers to folder actions

4. **Permission Enforcement**:
   - `pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go` - Guard middleware for permission validation
   - `pkg/services/accesscontrol/accesscontrol.go` - Core PermissionsService interfaces

5. **Permission Management APIs**:
   - `pkg/api/dashboard_permission.go` - Dashboard permission endpoints
   - `pkg/api/folder_permission.go` - Folder permission endpoints

6. **Permission Inheritance**: `pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go` - The `GetInheritedScopes()` function that implements folder hierarchy permission inheritance

The answer.json file includes 32 key symbols and explains the complete flow from role definitions through enforcement to inheritance across folder hierarchies.

  "text": "The Grafana user permission model implements a three-tier role-based access control (RBAC) system with Viewer, Editor, and Admin roles. Here is the comprehensive architecture:\n\n## Role Definition Hierarchy\nThe base role definitions are in `pkg/apimachinery/identity/role_type.go` which defines the `RoleType` enum with four values: RoleNone, RoleViewer, RoleEditor, and RoleAdmin. These are re-exported in `pkg/services/org/model.go` for organizational context.\n\n## Permission Types\n`pkg/services/dashboards/dashboardaccess/dashboard_access.go` defines three permission levels as bit flags:\n- PERMISSION_VIEW (1 << 0) - Read-only access\n- PERMISSION_EDIT (1 << 1) - Write/modify access\n- PERMISSION_ADMIN (1 << 2) - Admin access including permission management\n\n## ACL Models\n`pkg/services/dashboards/models.go` contains the `DashboardACL` struct which maps these permission types to users, teams, and roles. Permission data flows through `pkg/api/dtos/acl.go` which defines `UpdateDashboardACLCommand` and related DTOs.\n\n## Action-to-Permission Mapping\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/models.go` defines action constants (ActionFoldersRead, ActionFoldersWrite, ActionDashboardsRead, ActionDashboardsWrite, etc.) that represent granular permission operations.\n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go` maps the three role tiers to sets of actions:\n- DashboardViewActions: includes ActionDashboardsRead\n- DashboardEditActions: extends ViewActions with ActionDashboardsWrite, ActionDashboardsDelete\n- DashboardAdminActions: extends EditActions with ActionDashboardsPermissionsRead, ActionDashboardsPermissionsWrite\n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go` follows the same pattern for folder permissions:\n- FolderViewActions: ActionFoldersRead\n- FolderEditActions: extends ViewActions with ActionFoldersWrite, ActionFoldersDelete\n- FolderAdminActions: extends EditActions with ActionFoldersPermissionsRead, ActionFoldersPermissionsWrite\n\n## Role Registration and Built-in Roles\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go` contains `GetBuiltinRoles()` which registers fixed roles for Admin, Editor, and Viewer with their associated permission sets. These are referenced throughout the codebase.\n\n## Permission Enforcement Middleware\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go` provides the `Middleware()` function that acts as a permission guard for HTTP endpoints. It evaluates whether a user has required permissions before allowing access.\n\n`pkg/middleware/auth.go` provides additional auth middleware functions like `SnapshotPublicModeOrCreate` and `SnapshotPublicModeOrDelete` that enforce specific permission checks.\n\n## Permissions Service Interface\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/accesscontrol.go` defines the `PermissionsService`, `FolderPermissionsService`, and `DashboardPermissionsService` interfaces. These services are implemented in `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/service.go` with methods:\n- SetUserPermission: assigns a permission to a specific user\n- SetTeamPermission: assigns a permission to a team\n- SetBuiltInRolePermission: assigns a permission to a built-in role (Viewer/Editor/Admin)\n\nThe API layer in `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/api.go` exposes HTTP endpoints for managing these permissions.\n\n## Folder and Dashboard Permissions APIs\n`pkg/api/dashboard_permission.go` and `pkg/api/folder_permission.go` provide HTTP endpoints to:\n- Get permissions (using folder/dashboard-specific permission service)\n- Update permissions with validation\n- Convert permission objects for API responses\n\n## Permission Inheritance\n`pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go` contains the critical `GetInheritedScopes()` function which implements permission inheritance from parent folders to child dashboards and subfolders. This function queries the folder service to get ancestors and builds a chain of inherited permission scopes.\n\n`pkg/services/searchV2/auth.go` uses inherited scopes for search result filtering, checking permissions across the folder hierarchy.\n\n`pkg/api/annotations.go` applies inherited scopes when resolving permissions for annotations linked to dashboards.\n\n## Folder Hierarchy Support\n`pkg/services/folder/service.go` and `pkg/services/folder/model.go` provide folder hierarchy operations. The folder service's `GetParents()` method is used by `GetInheritedScopes()` to walk up the folder tree and collect inherited permission scopes.\n\nThis architecture enables Grafana to:\n1. Define granular permission actions mapped to three role tiers\n2. Enforce permissions via middleware on all endpoints\n3. Allow per-resource permission overrides for users, teams, and basic roles\n4. Inherit permissions from parent folders to contained resources\n5. Validate and update permissions through a centralized permissions service"
}
